source_intake_record.raw_question_text: For the signals \( x(t) \) and \( y(t) \) shown in the <image>, \( z(t) = x(t) * y(t) \) is maximum at \( t = T_1 \). Then \( T_1 \) in seconds is ________ (Round off to the nearest integer).
extraction_notes: answer field is an option letter and was mapped to the corresponding choice text
image field contains an in-memory PIL image object, not a path or URL

image_dependency: question_text explicitly says the signals x(t) and y(t) are 'shown in the image'
image_manifest contains 1 existing PNG image and registry_passed=true
multi_step: the task asks for the time t=T1 at which z(t)=x(t)*y(t) is maximum, which typically requires interpreting both plotted signals and reasoning about convolution/overlap over time
the question combines visual signal reading with a derived quantity (maximum of convolution), indicating more than direct lookup
multiple-choice options are provided, but determining the correct option still appears to require intermediate reasoning rather than a single explicit textual fact
verifiability: answer_type is numeric and answer_text is a single value ('4')
the prompt asks to round to the nearest integer, so the expected output format is well constrained
choices A-D are provided, enabling straightforward answer checking
asset_registry_record shows question_present=true, answer_present=true, and registry_passed=true

decision_reasons: 题干、图像和答案资产齐全，registry_passed=true 且无 issues
图像依赖度高，图像文件存在，样本核心语义可完整恢复
可验证性高，答案为单个数字且题目提供了明确的输出约束
无风险标记，综合看具有较高收录价值
